name: bin-reorganization
description: Reorganize bin/ directory: move 35 implementation modules to src/ by concern, keep 15 entry points in bin/, update all require() paths systematically with self-healing

operations.name: move-and-organize
operations.description: Move 35 modules from bin/ to src/ subdirectories by concern, preserving functionality

operations.name: update-requires-systematically
operations.description: Walk codebase and update all require() calls to point to new locations with self-healing for broken references
process: For each moved file, find all files that require() it
Update the require() path based on relative directory distance
If a require() path breaks (MODULE_NOT_FOUND), attempt self-healing by:
1. Checking if the file exists in an alternative location
2. Creating a re-export stub if needed
3. Recording the issue for manual review if unrecoverable

operations.name: keep-entry-points-in-bin
operations.description: Ensure only 15 true CLI entry points remain in bin/
operations.action: Verify these are the only .js files remaining in bin/ after moves

phase_1_structural: All 35 files moved from bin/ to src/ by concern
Exactly 15 entry points remain in bin/
phase_2_functional: No MODULE_NOT_FOUND errors when requiring moved files
Entry points in bin/ can still load and execute
phase_3_semantic: File anchors still reflect single responsibility after move
All moved files maintain their governance and semantic intent

self_healing_strategy.on_module_not_found: Attempt to locate file in alternative locations and create re-export if needed
self_healing_strategy.on_require_cycle: Detect and report but don't auto-fix (requires human judgment)

rollback_plan.on_failure: Revert all file moves and require() updates to restore original state
rollback_plan.on_semantic_regression: If coherence drops >2%, trigger rollback and review before retry
